title: Auction Sales Hit Record at Spring Contemporary Sale
notes: Spring contemporary auction totals exceeded estimates by 40%. Blue-chip works by established post-war artists drove results above reserve. Secondary market demand concentrated in price tier above $500k. Emerging artist lots showed weak bidding with multiple buy-ins. Several lots carried guarantees from third-party irrevocable bids.

title: Gallery Closures Accelerate as Rent and Fair Costs Rise
notes: Mid-tier galleries reported unsustainable cost structures driven by art fair participation fees and rising urban rents. Several long-established galleries announced closure or downsizing. Galleries with primary market focus reported difficulty placing works above $50k without auction track record. Fair fatigue reported by dealers as contributing to diminishing collector attendance.
